paper_title: Elastic Weight Consolidation Improves the Robustness of Self-Supervised Learning Methods under Transfer
main_topic: Using Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) to improve the robustness of Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) methods during transfer to downstream tasks
problem_addressed: SSL representations are free from label-induced bias, but this changes during fine-tuning to downstream tasks
Models trained with empirical risk minimization (ERM) tend to be biased towards label population distributions that are disproportionately represented in the training dataset
Catastrophic forgetting occurs when models are trained sequentially, becoming biased towards the latest observed distribution
proposed_solution: Use Bayesian continual learning to mitigate drift by regularizing downstream tasks towards their robust initial representation from SSL pre-training

methodology.approach: Treat SSL pre-training and fine-tuning as two distinct sequential tasks
methodology.technique: Use Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) to keep fine-tuned representations close to their initial SSL values
methodology.regularization: EWC regularizes model parameters towards their optimal values on previous tasks using the Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) and the Laplace approximation
methodology.theoretical_framework: Consider the SSL model as a statistical Bayesian model with prior p(θ)
methodology.objective: Given two datasets (SSL and FT), estimate parameters θ while computing the posterior distribution p(θ|D_SSL, D_FT)

validation.fmi_validation: Validate the accuracy of the FIM by analyzing reverse transfer performance from CIFAR10 fine-tuning
validation.issue_identified: The FIM for ViT-B/16 is poorly conditioned, making it impossible to fully recover SSL performance on ImageNet1k
validation.solution_proposed: Propose a method to alleviate the issue with poorly conditioned FIM
validation.results: With ViT-B/16, this regularization can improve worst group accuracy on Waterbirds by 5% and on Celeb-A by 2%

technical_details.models_used: DINO models pre-trained with ViT-B/16 and ResNet50 architectures
technical_details.fmi_computation: Compute the FIM on a subset of ImageNet1k images
technical_details.fmi_availability: The FIM is available at https://coming_soon

title: Elastic Weight Consolidation Improves the Robustness of Self-Supervised Learning Methods under Transfer
